title: The Denial of Death
author: Ernest Becker
excerpt: A Pulitzer Prize-winning synthesis of Kierkegaard, Rank, Freud, and Fromm arguing that the fear of death is the fundamental driver of human behavior — that civilization itself is an elaborate defense mechanism against mortality, and that our immortality projects, hero systems, and cultural worldviews are all desperate attempts to deny the one inescapable truth of existence.
